zh.lifeSummary: 當John Joseph Moore Jr.於1609年4月1日出生在英國賀特福爾比曉普也斯 斯托特福德時，他的父親Joseph Morse32歲，母親Dorothy Barber32歲。他於大約1625年與Elizabeth Moore在英國結婚。他們至少是1個兒子和2個女兒的父母。他去世於1673年1月6日在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加馬薩瑟次 貝 可隆尼米德萊可素德波利享年63歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当John Joseph Moore Jr.于1609年4月1日出生在英国賀特福爾比曉普也斯 斯托特福德时，他的父亲Joseph Morse32岁，母亲Dorothy Barber32岁。他于大约1625年与Elizabeth Moore在英国结婚。他们至少是1个儿子和2个女儿的父母。他去世于1673年1月6日在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加馬薩瑟次 貝 可隆尼米德萊可素德波利享年63岁。

nameMeanings.name: Moore
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: from Middle English more ‘moor, marsh, fen’ (Old English mōr), hence a topographic name for someone who lived in such a place, or a habitational name from any of various places called with this word, as for example Moore in Cheshire or More in Shropshire.
English (of Norman origin): ethnic name from Old French more ‘Moor’, either someone from North Africa or, more often, a nickname for someone thought to resemble a Moor. Compare Morrell and Moreau .

English (of Norman origin): from the Middle English personal name More (Old French More, Maur, Latin Maurus), originally denoting either ‘Moor’ or someone with a swarthy complexion (compare Morrell , Morrin , Morris , and sense 2 above). There was a 6th-century Christian saint of this name.

variants.name: Moor
meanings: English, Irish, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Moore  .
German: variant of   Mohr  .

Dutch (also De Moor): nickname for a man of swarthy complexion, from Middle Dutch moor ‘Moor’ (from Latin maurus), or a topographic or habitational name referring to a house distinguished by the sign of a Moor. Compare De Moor .

variants.name: More
meanings: English: from Middle English more ‘moor, marsh, fen’ (Old English mōr), hence a topographic name for someone who lived in such a place, or a habitational name from any of various places called with this word, as for example Moore in Cheshire or More in Shropshire.
English (of Norman origin): ethnic name from Old French more ‘Moor’, either someone from North Africa or, more often, a nickname for someone thought to resemble a Moor. Compare Morrell and Moreau .

English (of Norman origin): from the Middle English personal name More (Old French More, Maur, Latin Maurus), originally denoting either ‘Moor’ or someone with a swarthy complexion (compare Morrell , Morrin , Morris , and sense 2 above). There was a 6th-century Christian saint of this name.

variants.name: Morrin
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Irish Dermot.
English (of Norman origin): from the Old French personal name Morin, a diminutive of More (Old French More, Maur, Latin Maurus); see Moore 2 and 3.
English: perhaps sometimes from the Middle English personal name Morwin (Old English Mōrwine, the first element of which is uncertain and the second wine ‘friend’).

variants.name: Mauro
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Salvatore, Angelo, Sal, Domenic, Giuseppe, Antonio, Carmine, Carmelo, Domenico, Gaetano, Nino, Pasquale.

Italian (mainly southern): from the personal name Mauro, derived from the Latin personal and ethnic name Maurus, borne by various early Christian saints and meaning ‘Moor, North African’. Compare Moore and Moro .

variants.name: Moresco
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Primo, Gino, Luciano, Riccardo.
Italian:
(Morésco): from a medieval personal name or a nickname for someone from North Africa or a swarthy or dark-haired man, from medieval Latin Mauriscus ‘Moor’ (see Moore 2).

variants.name: Morey
meanings: Irish (Limerick and Cork): variant of   Moore   4.
English (of Norman origin): from the Old French personal name Mory, a pet form of Amauri (see Amery ).
English: perhaps also a variant of   Moore   1–3.

variants.name: Morrell
meanings: English (northern): from the medieval personal name Morel, a diminutive of Middle English or Old French More. Compare Moore .
Americanized form of French   Morel  . Compare   Morell   3.

variants.name: Moores
meanings: English (Lancashire and Cheshire): variant of Moore , with post-medieval excrescent -s.

variants.name: McMoore
meanings: Irish: variant of Moore 4, in which Mac- ‘son of’ has been prefixed in modern times to give the name (Ó Mórdha ‘descendant of Mórdha’) an unmistakably Gaelic identity.

variants.name: Moro
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Jose, Carlos, Mario, Alberto, Jorge, Lino, Luis, Luiz, Manuel, Ramiro, Adolfo, Aida.  Italian Angelo, Pino, Pio, Remo, Antonio, Ciro, Cosmo, Elio, Enrico, Geno, Giancarlo, Gino.
Spanish: nickname or ethnic name from moro ‘Moor’, from Latin Maurus ‘Moor, North African’.
Italian: nickname from moro ‘Moor’ (see Moore 2, compare 1 above), also ‘black-haired man’.

nameMeanings.name: John
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: New Testament: ‘God is gracious’. See John .

nameMeanings.name: Joseph
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: form of the biblical Hebrew name Yosef, meaning ‘(God) shall add (another son)’. This was borne by the favourite son of Jacob, whose brothers became jealous of him and sold him into slavery (Genesis 37). He was taken to Egypt, where he rose to become chief steward to Pharaoh, and was eventually reconciled to his brothers when they came to buy corn during a seven-year famine (Genesis 43–7). In the New Testament Joseph is the name of the husband of the Virgin Mary. It is also borne by a rich Jew, Joseph of Arimathea (Matthew 27:57; Mark 15:43; Luke 23:50; John 19:38), who took Jesus down from the Cross, wrapped him in a shroud, and buried him in a rock tomb. According to medieval legend, Joseph of Arimathea brought the Holy Grail to Britain. The name was uncommon in Britain in the Middle Ages but was revived in the mid 16th century and had become popular by the 1630s, remaining so ever since.

variants.name: Josef
meanings: , Dutch, Scandinavian, and Czech spelling of   Joseph  , now also in use in the English‐speaking world.

historicEvents.title: The Pilgrims Board the Mayflower
historicEvents.description: "The Pilgrims boarded the Mayflower in search of religious freedom in the New World in September 1620. The Mayflower set sail from Plymouth, England, with 102 passengers, 40 of whom called themselves ""Saints."" After two stormy months at sea, the ship reached the New World. Nearly half of the group died during their first winter due to malnutrition and the harsh New England winter."

historicEvents.title: The English Civil War
historicEvents.description: A series of conflicts regarding England's governance during the years 1642 to 1651 is now known as The English Civil War. Charles I summoned supporters to join him against his enemies in Parliament. In October 1642, nearly 10,000 men fought for Charles I and chased Parliament across the River Tamar. Fighting continued for years and was finally ended at the Battle of Worcester on September 3, 1651, with a Parliamentarian victory.

en.lifeSummary: When John Joseph Moore Jr. was born on 1 April 1609, in Bishop's Stortford, Hertfordshire, England, his father, Joseph Morse, was 32 and his mother, Dorothy Barber, was 32. He married Elizabeth Moore about 1625, in England. They were the parents of at least 1 son and 2 daughters. He died on 6 January 1673, in Sudbury, Middlesex, Massachusetts Bay Colony, British Colonial America, at the age of 63.

Il a épousé Elizabeth Moore environ 1625, à Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fils et 2 filles. Il est décédé le 6 janvier 1673, à Sudbury, Middlesex, Massachusetts Bay Colony, Amérique coloniale britannique, à l’âge de 63 ans.

it.lifeSummary: Quando John Joseph Moore Jr. è nato il il 1 aprile 1609 nel Bishop's Stortford, Hertfordshire, England, il suo padre, Joseph Morse, aveva 32 anni e il suo madre, Dorothy Barber, aveva 32 anni. Lui sposato Elizabeth Moore circa 1625, nel England. Erano genitori di almeno 1 figlio e 2 figlie. Morì il 6 gennaio 1673, nel Sudbury, Middlesex, Massachusetts Bay Colony, British Colonial America, all'età di 63 anni.

ko.lifeSummary: John Joseph Moore Jr.(이)가 1609년 4월 1일 영국 허트퍼드실르 비숍'스 스토르트퍼드에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Joseph Morse(은)는 32세였고, 그의 어머니 Dorothy Barber(은)는 32세였다. 그는 대략 1625년 영국에서 Elizabeth Moore(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 아들과 두 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1673년 1월 6일 브리티시 콜론이앨 어머릭아 마작세츠 베이 콜론이 미들레세스 수드버이에서 63세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando John Joseph Moore Jr. nasceu em 1 de abril de 1609, em Bishop's Stortford, Hertfordshire, Inglaterra, seu pai, Joseph Morse, tinha 32 anos e sua mãe, Dorothy Barber, tinha 32 anos. Ele casou-se com Elizabeth Moore aproximadamente 1625, em Inglaterra. Eles tiveram pelo menos 1 filho e 2 filhas. Ele faleceu em 6 de janeiro de 1673, em Sudbury, Middlesex, Massachusetts Bay Colony, British Colonial America, com 63 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда John Joseph Moore Jr. родился 1 апреля 1609 г. в местности бишоп'с стортфорд, хиртфордшир, Англия, его отцу по имени Joseph Morse было 32 года, а матери, которую звали Dorothy Barber, 32. Он женился на женщине по имени Elizabeth Moore около 1625 г. в местности Англия. У них был по крайней мере 1 сын и 2 дочери. Он умер 6 января 1673 г. в местности судбури, миддлесекс, массачусетц беи колони, бритиш колоняль амирика в возрасте 63 лет.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando John Joseph Moore Jr. nació el 1 de abril de 1609, en Bishop's Stortford, Hertfordshire, Inglaterra, su papá, Joseph Morse, tenía 32 años y su mamá, Dorothy Barber, tenía 32 años. Se casó con Elizabeth Moore aproximadamente 1625, en Inglaterra. Fueron padres de por lo menos 1 hijo y 2 hijas. Murió el 6 de enero de 1673, en Sudbury, Middlesex, Massachusetts Bay Colony, British Colonial America, a la edad de 63 años.
